definition: The influx of financial capital into commodity markets structurally broke the commodity-equity diversification benefit that underpinned the endowment model. A Bai-Perron structural break at 2004Q2 (t-stat 5.1) shows rolling 36-month correlations jumped from mean +0.03 (1990-2003) to +0.48 (2004-2020). Partial reversion to +0.28 (2021-2025) is statistically insufficient to declare a second regime change.
evidence: Bai-Perron structural break identified at 2004Q2 with t-stat 5.1
Commodity index AUM grew from ~$15B (2003) to $450B+ (2022) and has not reversed
Partial reversion to +0.28 insufficient for second regime change
Pair_2 confirmed as among the strongest empirical findings
open_questions: Would a sustained physical tightening episode restore diversification properties?
Has the commodity-equity correlation embedded a permanent regime via passive allocation?
